---
title: Streamlit
lightbox: true
---

[Streamlit](https://streamlit.io/) provides a simple, script-based approach to building data apps. This guide shows you how to integrate querychat into your Streamlit applications.

![](/images/streamlit-app.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of querychat running in a Streamlit app." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## Installation

Install querychat with Streamlit support:
```bash
pip install "querychat[streamlit]"
```

## Quick start

The simplest way to get started is with `.app()`, which creates a complete app with sidebar chat and main content area:

```{.python filename="streamlit-app.py"}
from querychat.streamlit import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")
qc.app()
```

Run with:
```bash
streamlit run streamlit-app.py
```

## State access

querychat provides three methods to access the current query state:

| Method | Returns | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `.df()` | DataFrame | Current filtered/sorted data |
| `.sql()` | str \| None | Current SQL query |
| `.title()` | str \| None | Short description of current filter |

These read directly from Streamlit's session state and update automatically when users interact with the chat.

```python
import streamlit as st
from querychat.streamlit import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")
qc.sidebar()

# Access current state
df = qc.df()           # Filtered DataFrame
sql = qc.sql()         # SQL query string
title = qc.title()     # Filter description

st.header(title or "All Data")
st.dataframe(df)
```

## Custom layouts

For more control over your app's layout, use `.sidebar()` or `.ui()`:

- **`.sidebar()`** - Renders chat in Streamlit's sidebar
- **`.ui()`** - Renders just the chat component (place anywhere)

### Sidebar layout

```{.python filename="streamlit-sidebar.py"}
import streamlit as st
from querychat.streamlit import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

st.set_page_config(layout="wide")

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")
qc.sidebar()  # Chat appears in sidebar

# Main content area
st.header(qc.title() or "Titanic Dataset")

col1, col2 = st.columns(2)
with col1:
    st.subheader("Data")
    st.dataframe(qc.df())

with col2:
    st.subheader("SQL Query")
    st.code(qc.sql() or "SELECT * FROM titanic", language="sql")
```

![](/images/streamlit-sidebar.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of Streamlit sidebar layout." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

### Custom placement

Use `.ui()` to place the chat anywhere in your layout:

```{.python filename="streamlit-custom.py"}
import streamlit as st
from querychat.streamlit import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")

col1, col2 = st.columns([1, 2])

with col1:
    st.header("Chat")
    qc.ui()  # Chat in left column

with col2:
    st.header(qc.title() or "Data View")
    st.dataframe(qc.df())
```

![](/images/streamlit-custom.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of Streamlit custom placement layout." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## Data views

Since `.df()` returns a standard DataFrame, you can use it with any Streamlit visualization:

```python
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px
from querychat.streamlit import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")
qc.sidebar()

st.header(qc.title() or "Titanic Explorer")

# Data table
st.dataframe(qc.df())

# Visualization
df = qc.df().to_pandas()  # Convert for plotly
fig = px.histogram(df, x="age", color="survived", title="Age Distribution")
st.plotly_chart(fig)
```

When users filter data through the chat (e.g., "show only survivors"), both the table and chart update automatically on the next Streamlit rerun.

## Reset functionality

Add a reset button to clear filters:

```python
import streamlit as st
from querychat.streamlit import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")

with st.sidebar:
    qc.ui()
    if st.button("Reset Filters"):
        qc.reset()
        st.rerun()

st.dataframe(qc.df())
```

## Complete example

Here's a full example combining all the concepts:

```{.python filename="streamlit-complete.py"}
import streamlit as st
import plotly.express as px
from querychat.streamlit import QueryChat
from querychat.data import titanic

st.set_page_config(page_title="Titanic Explorer", layout="wide")

qc = QueryChat(titanic(), "titanic")

# Sidebar with chat and reset
with st.sidebar:
    qc.ui()
    st.divider()
    if st.button("Reset Filters", use_container_width=True):
        qc.reset()
        st.rerun()

# Main content
st.header(qc.title() or "Titanic Dataset")

# Metrics row
df = qc.df().to_pandas()
col1, col2, col3 = st.columns(3)
col1.metric("Passengers", len(df))
col2.metric("Survivors", df["survived"].sum())
col3.metric("Survival Rate", f"{df['survived'].mean():.1%}")

# Data and visualization
tab1, tab2 = st.tabs(["Data", "Charts"])

with tab1:
    st.dataframe(df, use_container_width=True)
    st.caption(f"```sql\n{qc.sql() or 'SELECT * FROM titanic'}\n```")

with tab2:
    fig = px.histogram(df, x="age", color="survived",
                       title="Age Distribution by Survival")
    st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)
```

![](/images/streamlit-complete.png){fig-alt="Screenshot of complete Streamlit app with querychat." class="lightbox shadow rounded mb-3"}

## See also

- [Greet users](greet.qmd) - Create welcoming onboarding experiences
- [Provide context](context.qmd) - Help the LLM understand your data better
